verdict.answer_first: B states the actionable answer near the top: "Library: `react-i18next` for a Vite/CRA SPA, `next-intl` if you're on Next.js App Router"; A opens with a repo caveat instead.
verdict.decisiveness: B gives sensible defaults and a sequence; A also recommends `react-i18next`, but dilutes the answer with an ungrounded repo-specific preface.
verdict.fabrication: A invents context not in the question: "the app isn't in this directory — `/Users/elon/work/fable-profile`..."; B flags assumptions instead of presenting them as facts.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on: "Automate extraction", "Pseudo-localization", "One namespace, end-to-end first", plus a concrete sequencing line.
